claim.text: Structural Engineer required for Type III-A construction
claim.context: Type III-A construction requires engineered structure; 5-story mixed-use building exceeds prescriptive construction limits

verdicts.note: While Type III-A construction is a recognized classification and Denver requires structural engineering for various projects, the specific claim that "Structural Engineer required for Type III-A construction" could not be verified against authoritative Denver building code sources accessible online.

claim.text: IBC requires engineered HVAC systems for R-2 occupancy
claim.context: IBC requires engineered HVAC systems for R-2 occupancy and commercial spaces; mixed-use requires separate systems coordination

verdicts.note: The IBC does not universally require engineered HVAC systems for R-2 occupancy; rather, engineered systems are permitted as an exception to standard ventilation requirements, and no authoritative Denver source confirms this as a mandatory requirement for the jurisdiction.

claim.text: MEP Engineer - Mechanical required for R-2 occupancy and commercial spaces
claim.context: IBC requires engineered HVAC systems for R-2 occupancy and commercial spaces; mixed-use requires separate systems coordination

verdicts.note: While Denver's building code references R-2 occupancy requirements and requires sealed drawings for certain mechanical systems, the search results do not contain explicit confirmation of the specific claim that MEP Engineer - Mechanical is required for all R-2 occupancy and commercial spaces.

claim.text: MEP Engineer - Electrical required for commercial and residential systems
claim.context: Commercial and residential electrical systems require professional design; NEC compliance mandatory

verdicts.note: The specific designation "MEP Engineer - Electrical" does not appear in Denver's official building code documents; Denver uses "licensed professional electrical engineer" or "professional engineer" instead.

claim.text: IBC Section 508 requires fire separation analysis for mixed-use occupancies
claim.context: Mixed-use occupancies per IBC Section 508 require fire separation analysis; sprinkler systems likely required

verdicts.note: IBC Section 508 is a real, currently-used code section that explicitly addresses mixed-use occupancies and requires fire separation analysis through Table 508.4 and related provisions, and Denver has adopted this section in its building code.

claim.text: Civil Engineer required for site development and stormwater management
claim.context: Site development, utilities, stormwater management required for 12,000 SF lot development

verdicts.note: Denver's authoritative stormwater and site development standards explicitly require Professional Engineer (PE) preparation and stamping of stormwater management plans and civil drawings for site development.

claim.text: Geotechnical Engineer required for Type III-A construction on 5-story building
claim.context: Foundation design requires soil analysis; Type III-A construction on 5-story building

verdicts.note: Denver building code sources do not contain a specific requirement mandating a geotechnical engineer for Type III-A construction on 5-story buildings; geotechnical requirements are triggered by specific conditions (deep foundations, excavation, soil type) rather than construction type alone.

claim.text: Denver mixed-use zoning has landscaping requirements
claim.context: Denver mixed-use zoning has landscaping requirements; pedestrian-friendly environment standards

verdicts.note: Denver's zoning code (Article 10, Division 10.5) establishes landscaping standards that apply to mixed-use developments, and the code explicitly requires landscaping material standards and pedestrian-friendly environment provisions for mixed-use zone districts.

claim.context: 8,000 SF retail may include restaurant/food service requiring specialized design

verdicts.note: While Denver regulations do allow retail spaces to include food service with specialized design requirements, no authoritative source explicitly confirms the specific "8,000 SF" threshold mentioned in the claim.

claim.text: Denver zoning has shielding requirements for lighting
claim.context: Denver zoning has shielding requirements

verdicts.note: Denver zoning code Section 10.2.3.8 explicitly requires shielding of parking lot illumination from adjacent residential districts, confirming the claim.

claim.text: IBC Section 508 requires fire-resistance rated construction between occupancy groups
claim.context: IBC Section 508 requires fire-resistance rated construction between occupancy groups

verdicts.note: The claim is imprecise and misleading—IBC Section 508.4 (not Section 508 generally) requires fire-resistance-rated construction between separated occupancies, while Section 508.3 (nonseparated occupancies) explicitly requires no separation.

claim.text: Denver zoning requires General Development Plan (GDP) review with 21-day Development Review Committee process
claim.context: Project may require General Development Plan (GDP) review with 21-day Development Review Committee process

verdicts.note: The claim conflates the 21-day Planning Board hearing timeline with a "Development Review Committee process" — the 21-day period refers to the minimum time before a Planning Board hearing, not a DRC review timeline, and the GDP process has been largely replaced by the LDR process since 2019.

claim.text: 21-day Development Review Committee process for GDP
claim.context: Project may require General Development Plan (GDP) review with 21-day Development Review Committee process

verdicts.note: Denver's official GDP Rules and Regulations document confirms a 21-day period for Development Review Committee staff to provide comments on GDP applications.
